they are very troubled because all these things trouble them so after some time they can't stand it they have to disrobe so this is especially difficult now for people with strong sensual desires there are some people with strong sensual desires and they find it very difficult to overcome especially when they uh they see the opposite section um the only way uh the buddha said that we can overcome these type of desires and other types is strong samadhi um in fact there is 1 suta in the majima nikaya called the nibapa and in that suta the buddha gave a simile and likening monks to a deer a flop of deer they have to find food to eat if they stay deep into the jungle it's quite difficult for these deer to survive so they they try to get their food from the farmers crops but if they come to near the farmer's crops the farmer will trap them and kill them if they stay too far they get hungry so they try to take the middle path not too far and not too near so the buddha said the same way a monk has to live near lay people to get the support but he must also keep his distance and strong samadhi will help a lot in this case so these are the 4 types of uh monks who disrobe for another thing you want to notice here in this uttar is that the warfu planes are mentioned here are only 3 always in the sutasana we only find 3 woeful planes hell animal realm and the ghost realm whereas in the later books like the abhidharma and the mahayana books they mentioned 4 woeful planes include the asuras whereas the asuras are a kind of deva this you can get confirmation in the deega
